title: Incomplete context to first useful diagnostic question
turns: we're 1584 Design. We do premium STR and second-home interior design projects, mostly full-service design, procurement, and install.
most leads come from referrals and Instagram. I don't know CAC exactly yet.
what should we figure out first?
success_rubric: Agent inspects or asks for enough business context before diagnosing.
Agent saves clear business facts to the snapshot when provided.
Agent does not search source material just to compensate for missing economics.
Agent asks for the next needed economics field instead of making a premature recommendation.
Every turn is recorded with session finish.
failure_modes: Gives generic money-model advice without using saved context.
Recommends a fix before CAC or first-30-day gross profit is known.
Searches source material when the immediate need is missing business facts.
Fails to persist clear facts from the conversation.
notes: Tests whether the advisor can move from vague opening to useful missing-context collection without over-searching.

title: Numbers arrive gradually and require calculation plus diagnosis
turns: I think our average referral partner cost is about 2400 for every 3 signed projects.
Our diagnostic would sell for 997 and cost maybe 150 to fulfill.
Full-service projects produce around 10000 in first-month gross profit on average.
so does that mean paid acquisition is viable or is CAC still the bottleneck?
success_rubric: Agent calculates CAC from spend and signed projects.
Agent calculates or correctly uses first-30-day gross profit and gross margin when relevant.
Agent updates saved economics only for explicit user-provided or calculated facts.
Agent diagnoses whether CAC/payback is the likely bottleneck from saved numbers.
Agent searches source material only when supporting a source-backed diagnosis or recommendation.
Search source events include focused SourceNeeds, executed query variants, inspected chunks, and citations.
failure_modes: Does math in prose without calculation_events.
Fails to save CAC or other explicit economics.
Searches too early before enough economics exist.
Uses broad catch-all SourceNeeds instead of separated diagnostic/recommendation searches.
Gives generic advice not tied to 1584 economics.
notes: Tests the full stateful loop: update snapshot, calculate, diagnose, search, cite, and recommend.

title: Recommendation challenge and source-backed explanation
turns: why not add a recurring owner maintenance offer first?
explain the STR Design Diagnostic as a possible front-end/attraction offer in plain English, but cite the Money Models source material.
what would make you change your mind?
success_rubric: Agent uses existing diagnosed snapshot instead of re-asking known facts.
Agent makes a specific recommendation tied to 1584 context.
Agent can compare the candidate attraction/front-end offer vs continuity without collapsing them into one source need.
Agent searches source material for source-backed teaching/comparison/recommendation turns.
Agent cites chunks that plausibly support the claims being made.
Agent answers the challenge directly and identifies decision criteria that would change the recommendation.
failure_modes: Ignores the user's challenge and repeats the same answer.
Over-searches unit economics merely because known economics appear in the answer.
Fails to source the front-end/attraction-offer explanation when citations are requested.
Uses citations that do not support the recommendation.
Does not preserve complete traces across turns.
notes: Tests product usefulness under challenge, not just happy-path mechanics.
